title: For the Beauty of the Earth
author: Folliot S. Pierpoint
story: Pierpoint wrote this at age 29, inspired by the beautiful countryside around Bath, England. Originally a communion hymn, its grateful celebration of creation has made it one of the most beloved hymns of thanksgiving.

lines: For the beauty of the earth For the glory of the skies, Friends on earth and friends above, For all gentle thoughts and mild
For Thy virgins' robes of snow, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
For the beauty of each hour, Of the day and of the night, Off'ring up on every shore Her pure sacrifice of love
For Thy maiden mother mild, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
For the joy of ear and eye, For the heart and mind's delight, Lord of all, to Thee we raise, This our hymn of grateful praise
For Thyself, with hearts aglow, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
For the joy of human love, Brother, sister, parent, child, 6
For the martyrs' crown of light, Jesu, Victim undefiled
Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
For Thy Church, that evermore Lifteth holy hands above
For Thy prophets' eagle eye, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven.
For the love which from our birth Over and around us lies
For each perfect gift of Thine, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
Hill and vale, and tree and flow'r, Sun and moon, and stars of light
To our race so freely given, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
For the mystic harmony Linking sense to sound and sight
Graces human and divine, Flowers of earth and buds of Heaven
